questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are imaginary cube roots of units then value of $\alpha^4+\beta^{28}+\frac{1}{\alpha \beta}$ is
questions.solution:
Let $\alpha=\omega, \beta=\omega^2$
Now $\alpha^4+\beta^{28}+\frac{1}{\alpha \beta}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\omega^4+\left(\omega^2\right)^{28}+\frac{1}{\omega \omega^2} \\ & =\omega^4+\omega^{56}+\frac{1}{\omega^3} \\ & =\omega+\omega^2+\frac{1}{1} \\ & \left.=0 \quad \quad \quad \text { [as } \omega^3=1\right]\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The points in the set $\left\{z \in C: \arg \left(\frac{z-2}{z-6 i}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}\right\}$ (where $C$ denotes the set of all complex numbers) lie on the curve which is a
questions.solution:
Given that, $\arg \left(\frac{z-2}{z-6 i}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}$
$$
\therefore \quad \arg (z-2)-\arg (z-6 i)=\frac{\pi}{2}
$$
Let $z=x+i y$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \arg [(x-2)+i y]-\arg [x+i(y-6)]=\frac{\pi}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \tan ^{-1} \frac{y}{x-2}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{y-6}{x}=\frac{\pi}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad\left(\frac{\frac{y}{x-2}-\frac{y-6}{x}}{1+\frac{y}{x-2} \cdot \frac{y-6}{x}}\right)=\tan \frac{\pi}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 1+\frac{y}{x-2} \cdot \frac{y-6}{x}=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x(x-2)+y(y-6)=0
\end{aligned}
$$
This is an equation of circle in diametric form.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: If <math><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>β</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>C</mi></math> are the distinct roots of the equation <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><msup><mrow><mi>α</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>101</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>β</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>107</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to

x2-x+1=0

Roots are -ω, -ω2

α101+β107=-ω101+-ω2107

=-ω2-ω

=1

questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>99</mn></munderover><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>!</mo></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">ω</mi><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>!</mo></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> is (where, <math><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">ω</mi></math> is non-real cube root of unity)
questions.solution:
∑k=199ik!+∑k=199ωk!
∑k=199ik!=i1!+i2!+i3!+i4!+…+i99!
=i-1+i6+1+1+1+…+1
=i-2+96=i+94
∑k=199ωk!=ω1+ω2!+ω3!+ω4!+…+ω99!
=ω+ω2+1+1+1+…+1=96
Sum =i+94+96=i+190
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For a complex number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi></math>, if <math><msup><mrow><mi>z</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>z</mi></mrow><mo>-</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>i</mi></math> and <math><mi>z</mi></math> does not lie in the first quadrant, then (where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>i</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math>)
questions.solution:
Put z=x+iy
⇒x2-y2+2ixy-2iy=4i
⇒x2-y2=0⇒x=y or -yxy-y=2
Case i if x=y, then x2-x-2=0
⇒x=-1,2
⇒z1=-1-iz2=2+2i rejected
Case ii if x=-y, then x2-x+2=0
⇒x∈ϕ

Hence, z=-1-i=2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>i</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>i</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo></math>
questions.solution:
1z1+2z2z3z2

=11-2i+21+iz3z2

=1+2i1-2i1+2i+21-i1+i1-i32+4212+12

=1+2i5+2+2i252

=5215+2i5+1-i

=126-3i=452
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The expression <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>α</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>i</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>α</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>i</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> when simplified reduces to
questions.solution:
Let 1+itanα1-itanαn-1+itannα1-itannα=z

We know that tanα=sinαcosα

⇒z=cosα+isinαcosα-isinαn-cosnα+isinnαcosnα-isinnα

From Euler formula, we get

⇒z=eiαe-iαn-einαe-inα

⇒z=e2iαn-e2inα=e2inα-e2inα=0

∴1+itanα1-itanαn-1+itannα1-itannα=0
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math><mi mathvariant="normal">z</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">w</mi></math> be non-zero complex numbers such that <math><mi mathvariant="normal">z</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">w</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">z</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">z</mi><mo>-</mo><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">z</mi></mrow><mo>-</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">w</mi><mo>+</mo><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">w</mi></mrow><mo>-</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If <math><mi mathvariant="normal">w</mi></math> varies, then the perimeter of the locus of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">z</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Given, zw=z2⇒zw=zz-
⇒w=z- z≠0
Now, z-z-+w+w-=4
⇒z-z-+z+z-=4
Let, z=x+iy, then we get,
x+y=2
which represents a square of side length equal to 22

⇒ The perimeter of the locus is 82 units
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\omega$ is a complex cube root of unity, then $225+\left(3 \omega+8 \omega^2\right)^2+\left(3 \omega^2+8 \omega\right)^2$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$225+\left(3 \omega+8 \omega^2\right)^2+\left(3 \omega^2+8 \omega\right)^2$
$=225+9 \omega^2+64 \omega^4+48 \omega^3+9 \omega^4+64 \omega^2+48 \omega^3$
$=225+9 \omega^2+64 \omega+48+9 \omega+64 \omega^2+48$
$=225+73\left(\omega^2+\omega\right)+96$
$=225-73+96=225+23=248$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For a complex number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi></math>, the product of the real parts of the roots of the equation <math><msup><mrow><mi>z</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>i</mi></math> is (where, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></math>)
questions.solution:
Equation is z2-z-5+5i=0
⇒z=1±1+20-20i2
⇒z=1±5-2i22
⇒z=1±5-2i2=3-i,-2+i
The required value =-6
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $z=x+i y$ is a complex number satisfying $\left|z+\frac{i}{2}\right|^2=\left|z-\frac{i}{2}\right|^2$, then the locus of $z$ is
questions.solution:
We have,
$$
\left|z+\frac{i}{2}\right|^2=\left|z-\frac{i}{2}\right|^2
$$
$$
\begin{array}{rlrl}
\Rightarrow & \left|x+i y+\frac{i}{2}\right|^2 & =\left|x+i y-\frac{i}{2}\right|^2 \\
\Rightarrow & \left|x+i\left(y+\frac{1}{2}\right)\right|^2 & =\left|x+i\left(y+\frac{1}{2}\right)\right|^2 \\
\Rightarrow & x^2+\left(y+\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 & =x^2+\left(y-\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 \\
\Rightarrow & x^2+y^2+\frac{1}{4}+y & =x^2+y^2+\frac{1}{4}-y \\
\Rightarrow & & 2 y & =0 \\
\Rightarrow & y & =0
\end{array}
$$
Locus of $z$ is $x$-axis.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $A, B, C$ be three sets of complex numbers as defined below. $A=\{z ; \operatorname{lm} z \geq 1\} ; B=\{z:|z-2-i|=3\} ; C=\{z: \operatorname{Re}((1-i) z)=\sqrt{2}\}$.Question: The number of elements in the set $A \cap B \cap C$ is
questions.solution:
Let $z=x+i y$ Set $A$ corresponds to the region $y \geq 1$. Set $B$ consists of points lying on the circle, centred at $(2,1)$ and radius 3 , i.e., $x^2+y^2-4 x-2 y=4$ $$ \text { Set } C \text { consists of points lying on the } x+y=\sqrt{2} $$
Clearly, there is only one point of intersection of the line $$ x+y=\sqrt{2} \text { and circle } x^2+y^2-4 x-2 y=4 . $$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
If z and ω are two complex numbers such that zω=1 and argz-argω=3π2, then arg1-2z¯ω1+3z¯ω is:
(Here argz denotes the principal argument of complex number z)
questions.solution:
As

zω=1

If z=r, then ω=1r

Let argz=θ

∴ argω=θ-3π2

So,

z=reiθ

⇒z¯=rei-θ
⇒ω=1reiθ-3π2

Now, consider
1-2z¯ω1+3z¯ω=1-2ei-3π21+3ei-3π2=1-2i1+3i

=1-2i1-3i1+3i1-3i=1-5i+6i210=-5+5i10=-121+i

Then,

principal arg1-2z¯ω1+3z¯ω

=principal arg-121+i

=-π-π4=-3π4
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ω</mi><mo> </mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>ω</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is a cube root of unity, such that <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>ω</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>ω</mi></math> where <math><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>b</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
-ω8=a+bω
⇒ω2=a+bω………i
⇒-12-i32=a+b-12+i32
Comparing real and imaginary parts
a=-1,b=-1⇒a+b=2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mi> </mi></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> are two complex numbers such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>></mo><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi></math> then
questions.solution:
Given that z1=z2+z1-z2, z1>z2

∴ z1-z2=z1-z2

                      =z1-z2

Now,  argz1=argz2

⇒arg⁡z1z2=0

⇒Imz1z2=0.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The least value of z where z is complex number which satisfies the inequality ez+3z-1z+1loge2≥log257+9i, i=-1, is equal to :
questions.solution:
ez+3z-1z+1loge2≥log257+9i

⇒2z+3z-1z+1≥log216

⇒2z+3z-1z+1≥23

z+3z-1z+1≥3

z+3z-1≥3z+1

z2+2z-3≥3z+3

⇒z2+z-6≥0

⇒z-3z+2≥0⇒z-3≥0

⇒z≥3  ⇒zmin=3
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> be two complex numbers such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>arg</mi><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> satisfy the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|"><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mo>Re</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>z</mi><mo>)</mo></math>. Then the imaginary part <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Let z1=x1+iy1,z2=x2+iy2
z1-z2=x1-x2+iy1-y2
∵argz1-z2=π4⇒tan-1y1-y2x1-x2=π4
⇒y1-y2=x1-x2----(1)
z1-3=Rez1⇒x1-32+y12=x12
z2-3=Rez2⇒x2-32+y22=x22
⇒x1-32-x2-32+y12-y22=x12-x22
⇒x1-x2x1+x2-6+y1-y2y1+y2=x1+x2x1-x2
⇒x1+x2-6+y1+y2=x1+x2
⇒y1+y2=6
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If z ≠ 1 and <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <msup> <mtext>z</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msup> </mrow> <mrow> <mtext>z</mtext> <mo>-</mo> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> </mfrac> </mrow> </math> is real, then the point represented by the complex number z lies
options: Either on the real axis or on a circle not passing through the origin.
Either on the real axis or on a circle passing through the origin.
On a circle with centre at the origin.
questions.solution:
z ≠ 1 ,  z 2 z - 1  is real

If z is a real number, then z 2 z - 1  is real.

Let z = x + iy

∴ x 2 - y 2 + 2 xiy x - 1 - iy x - 1 2 + y 2  equating imaginary part to zero.

⇒ - y x 2 - y 2 + 2 xy x - 1 = 0

⇒ y x 2 + y 2 - 2 x = 0 ⇒ y = 0  or  x 2 + y 2 - 2 x = 0

∴  z lies on real axis or on a circle passing through origin.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
